quick_facts.label: Degree level and purpose
quick_facts.value: A Ph.D. in Physiotherapy is a research doctorate undertaken under an approved research supervisor and culminating in original scholarly work; it is distinct from the practice-oriented MPT that normally precedes it.

quick_facts.label: Usual entry qualification
quick_facts.value: For this physiotherapy pathway, an MPT or equivalent relevant postgraduate qualification is ordinarily expected. The UGC baseline permits a qualifying master’s degree with at least 55% aggregate/equivalent grade, subject to applicable relaxations; individual universities may set discipline-specific requirements and higher thresholds.

quick_facts.label: Minimum duration
quick_facts.value: The UGC Ph.D. framework sets a minimum of 3 years, including coursework, and a normal maximum of 6 years from admission. Individual institutions can publish mode-specific minimums; for example, Integral University lists 3 years minimum full-time and 4 years minimum part-time for its Physiotherapy Ph.D.

quick_facts.label: Required doctoral coursework
quick_facts.value: UGC regulations require at least 12 coursework credits, including research methodology and Research and Publication Ethics; scholars also receive training related to teaching, education, pedagogy or writing in their Ph.D. subject.

quick_facts.label: Admission route
quick_facts.value: Admission is institution-specific but may use an HEI entrance test and interview/viva voce, or an interview route for candidates qualifying relevant national fellowship/scholarship tests. For an HEI test, UGC specifies 50% research methodology and 50% subject-specific content, with 70% test and 30% interview weightage for that selection route.

sections.heading: Eligibility after MPT
paragraphs: The usual progression is MPT to Ph.D. in Physiotherapy. At the national level, the UGC permits admission to a Ph.D. for candidates holding an eligible master’s degree with at least 55% aggregate marks or equivalent grade; it allows a 5-percentage-point relaxation for SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer)/Differently-Abled/EWS and other categories as decided by the Commission. The university offering the Physiotherapy Ph.D. determines the exact subject match, recognition/equivalence rules, and any higher cutoff.

A concrete programme example illustrates this institutional variation: Integral University lists an MPT from a recognised institute/university with 60% aggregate for both its full-time and part-time Ph.D. in Physiotherapy options. Applicants should therefore treat the UGC threshold as a baseline and check the current doctoral prospectus of each target university.
bullets: Prepare MPT marksheets and degree/provisional certificate, and confirm that the awarding institution and qualification are accepted by the target university.
Check category-relaxation, equivalence, employment/no-objection requirements for part-time registration, and supervisor/seat availability in the intended physiotherapy research area.
Match the proposed topic to available research supervision—for example, programmes may organise physiotherapy research around musculoskeletal/sports, neurological, or paediatric areas.

sections.heading: Duration and registration limits
paragraphs: Under the UGC Ph.D. Regulations, 2022, the doctorate has a minimum duration of 3 years, including coursework, and a maximum duration of 6 years from admission. An HEI may allow up to 2 additional years through re-registration under its own statute or ordinance, taking the usual absolute limit to 8 years.

Female scholars and persons with disabilities of more than 40% may receive up to a further 2 years’ relaxation, subject to a total limit of 10 years; the regulations also provide for up to 240 days of maternity/child-care leave during the programme. Delivery and registration mode are university-specific: Integral University, for example, publishes a 3-year minimum for full-time Physiotherapy Ph.D. registration and a 4-year minimum for part-time registration.
bullets: Plan for a multi-year sequence of coursework, proposal development, ethics and data-collection approvals where relevant, research execution, thesis writing, and examination.
Do not assume a part-time option, duration, or extension policy is identical across universities; verify the current research ordinance and department notice before applying.

sections.heading: What the doctoral work covers
paragraphs: This is a research-led degree rather than a fixed taught specialisation. The national minimum requires at least 12 Ph.D. coursework credits, specifically including research methodology and Research and Publication Ethics. The Research Advisory Committee may also recommend UGC-recognised online courses as part of the credit requirement.

Beyond coursework, the programme centres on supervised original research and a dissertation/thesis. The specific clinical or rehabilitation focus is set by the approved proposal, departmental expertise and supervisor availability. Published Physiotherapy Ph.D. offerings demonstrate possible focus areas such as musculoskeletal and sports disorders, neurophysiotherapy, and paediatric physiotherapy, but these should not be read as a universal syllabus.
bullets: Core doctoral preparation: research methodology, research and publication ethics, and discipline-relevant scholarly training.
Scholars must also train in teaching, education, pedagogy or writing related to their chosen Ph.D. subject; they may be assigned 4–6 hours per week of teaching or research assistantship.

Choose a department based on supervisor publications, ethical/clinical research infrastructure, access to participants or rehabilitation settings, and fit with the proposed question—not solely on the degree title.

sections.heading: Admission process
paragraphs: The UGC requires institutions to notify a doctoral prospectus in advance with seat numbers, subject-wise availability, criteria and procedure, and to follow the applicable national or state reservation policy. In practice, first identify departments offering Physiotherapy Ph.D. seats and supervisors whose areas match the proposed research question.

UGC permits HEIs to admit candidates qualifying relevant national-level fellowship/scholarship examinations—such as UGC-NET, UGC-CSIR NET, GATE or CEED—through an interview route. Alternatively, an HEI may conduct its own entrance test: its syllabus must be 50% research methodology and 50% subject-specific, candidates scoring 50% are eligible to be called for interview, and selection through this route uses 70% entrance-test and 30% interview/viva-voce weightage. Applicable category relaxation in entrance-test marks is 5 percentage points.

University implementation varies. For example, Sharda University’s published Allied Health Sciences page lists Physiotherapy among its Ph.D. specialisations and describes an application, entrance examination where required, and interview sequence; its displayed fees and application details are tied to the page’s stated 2025–26 context and should be reconfirmed for the intended admission cycle.
bullets: Shortlist current university notifications rather than relying on generic course listings.
Prepare a concise research proposal, academic records, identity/category documents where applicable, and evidence of national test/fellowship qualification if claimed.

Before paying an application fee, verify the current cycle’s seat matrix, supervisor availability, full-time/part-time rules, entrance-test exemption policy, fee schedule and refund conditions directly with the university.
